papers.title_en: Dynamic Rowhammer Threshold Management:Temperature-Aware Threshold Degradation for In-DRAM Defenses
papers.evidence: 动态Rowhammer阈值管理防御内核提权攻击。
papers.tldr: DRAM中Rowhammer防御的缓解阈值通常在制造时固定，但物理Rowhammer阈值随运行时温度变化。本文提出动态Rowhammer阈值管理层，一种与防御无关的运行时层，通过线性温度模型和VRD驱动的防护带g，每周期根据观测温度重新配置各防御的阈值参数，并投影到SALT-C、PRAC和TRR上。实验表明，在85°C时PRAC的72次陈旧度违规降至零；当σ=0.10时，防护带g从1.0降至0.9使PRAC违规从38.4降至9.6。SALT-C的违规从静态10次降至动态2次、启动0次，延迟开销≤5.1%。该层为温度自适应行锤防御提供了通用、高效的管理机制。
papers.abstract_en: In-DRAM Rowhammer defenses pin the mitigation threshold at manufacture time, yet the true Rowhammer Threshold (TRHD) varies with runtime temperature. We propose Dynamic Rowhammer Threshold Management, a defense-agnostic runtime layer that re-sources each defense's threshold from the observed temperature once per epoch via a linear-$T$ model with a VRD-motivated guardband $g$, projecting the result onto SALT-C, PRAC, and TRR through each defense's threshold parameter. A decoupled oracle that scales physical TRHD per-DIMM by $δ\sim \mathrm{N}(1, σ)$ breaks model self-consistency. The layer drives PRAC's 72 staleness breaches at 85$^\circ$C to zero; at $σ{=}0.10$, sweeping $g$ collapses PRAC breaches from 38.4 ($g{=}1.0$) to 9.6 ($g{=}0.9$). SALT-C drops from 10 nominal-static breaches to 2 (Dynamic) to 0 (bootstrap), at $\leq$5.1\% latency. TRR is capacity-limited; the layer acts as a diagnostic.

papers.title_en: A Measurement Study of AI-Environment Realism Gaps in Malware-Analysis Sandboxes
papers.authors: Zhiyong Sui, Lamine Noureddine, Mst Eshita Khatun, Sideeq Bello, Babangida Bappah, Justin Woodring, Aisha Ali-Gombe
papers.evidence: 系统化测量AI环境遗留作为沙箱逃避的新特征面
papers.tldr: 恶意软件常通过感知沙箱环境来逃避动态分析，传统逃避技术基于虚拟化硬件痕迹、时间扰动等，但随AI软件生态的普及，恶意软件开始依赖AI相关文件、配置、服务等痕迹来判断执行环境，构成新型逃避向量。本文提出AIprint探针框架，系统采集并总结AI环境遗留的持久性痕迹，涵盖AI助手配置目录、模型缓存、环境变量、本地推理服务及包依赖关系等，从284个开源AI项目提取450项独立痕迹，转化为非特权Windows探针。我们在7个主流商业及开源沙箱后端和3台AI配备的真实主机上测量，发现传统VM检测技术无法可靠区分，而12项AI环境痕迹仅在真实AI主机上存在，成为稳健指标。通过控制214步安装实验明确AI工具安装因果导致痕迹累积，同时基于自适应欺骗实验证明，伪造可信的AI环境成本远高于检测浅层欺骗，揭示了明显的防御不对称性。这项首次针对AI环境真实感差距的测量研究为强化沙箱对抗智能逃避提供了实证洞见和可行方向。
papers.abstract_en: Sandboxing remains a core technique for observing suspicious program behavior, yet environment-aware malware increasingly suppresses execution when analysis is suspected. Prior generations of sandbox evasion focused on virtualization artifacts, timing discrepancies, and wear-and-tear realism. In this paper, we present the first systematic measurement study of AI-environment artifacts as a new sandbox-evasion surface. We operationalize this realism gap through AIprint, a probe framework that captures persistent artifacts left behind by AI-capable software ecosystems, including AI-assistant configuration directories, model caches, environment variables, local inference services, and package dependencies.   We systematically extract 450 unique artifacts from 284 open-source AI projects on GitHub, compile them into unprivileged Windows probes, and evaluate them across seven commercial and open-source sandbox backends together with three AI-capable reference hosts. Our results show that traditional VM-detection baselines fail to reliably distinguish real AI-capable systems from modern sandboxes, whereas twelve AI-environment artifacts appear on the reference hosts and on none of the evaluated backends. A controlled 214-step installation experiment establishes a causal relationship between AI tool and package installation and measurable AI-environment artifact accumulation, while adaptive spoofing experiments reveal a fundamental operational asymmetry: reproducing convincing AI software environments is substantially more expensive than detecting shallow spoofing.

papers.title_en: Automated Template-free Synthesis of Instruction-Centric Leakage Contracts for Black-Box CPUs
papers.authors: Elvira Moreno, Tiziano Marinaro, Ryan Williams, Marco Patrignani, Roberto Guanciale, Hamed Nemati, Marco Guarnieri
papers.evidence: 通过观察CPU执行自动合成指令中心泄露合约，支持对微架构侧信道的动态分析
papers.tldr: 随着侧信道攻击日益进化，CPU微架构中的细微差异成为敏感信息泄露的渠道。近年来提出的泄漏合约作为ISA级安全抽象，为抵御此类攻击提供了理论基础，但手工设计合约耗时且难以覆盖复杂微架构，导致现代CPU普遍缺乏补全。针对此问题，本研究提出一种无模板自动化方法，直接从黑盒CPU合成以指令为中心的泄漏合约，并实现为工具malcos。通过x86与ARM处理器上的评估，malcos合成的合约展现高精度与可靠性，证明了从实际CPU学习泄漏合约的可行性，这一成果为简化安全CPU开发流程提供了可能。
papers.abstract_en: Side-channel attacks pose a significant security threat for modern computing platforms, because they exploit subtle discrepancies in CPU behaviors to leak sensitive information. To model the information leaked by a CPU via microarchitectural side-channels, recent work proposed leakage contracts: an ISA-level security abstraction that provides the foundations for secure CPU programming. Unfortunately, due to the complexity of current microarchitectures, devising a leakage contract for a CPU requires extensive manual effort and thus modern CPUs lack dedicated leakage contracts.   We present a methodology to extract instruction-centric leakage contracts for major CPU architectures with minimal manual intervention. We implemented this technique in malcos, the first template-free tool that automates the synthesis of leakage contracts for black-box CPUs. We evaluate malcos on x86 and ARM CPUs, and show that the contracts it synthesizes are precise and sound with respect to all leaks observed during synthesis. Our results demonstrate that learning leakage contracts from black-box CPUs is feasible.
